text: <par>The final stop probably arose via assimilation to the stop initial of the "infinitive nominalizer" <latinform>-pə</latinform>.</par>

<par>cf. <latinform>puam</latinform> above <emph>(ADD: VSTB 223: swollen spleen: note--add Lushai, etc.).(Use as Note: first gloss of </emph><latinform>spleen</latinform><emph>in Intro.: </emph><latinform>tla</latinform><emph>< WT *gla(y)/kla(y) (*g/k la-y) </emph><latinform>to drop/cause to fall/fall</latinform><emph>. Check 1.5.2. glosses: </emph><latinform>swollen</latinform><emph>: add from JAM Loloish p. 47 (#92) </emph><emph>all reflexes</emph><emph>)</emph></par>
<par>JAM-ety has <latinform>thôp</latinform>, with note: “assim of Cf to medial <latinform>-w-</latinform>” .</par>
<par>Apparently with assimilation of *-<latinform>k</latinform> to the labial medial *-<latinform>w</latinform>-.</par>
<par>Apparently with assimilation of <reconstruction>*-k</reconstruction> to the labial medial <reconstruction>*-w-</reconstruction>.</par>

<par>Contrast the last syllables of Meithei <latinform>thi-gun</latinform> 'anus' (< <xref ref="572">#572</xref> + <reconstruction>*kor</reconstruction>) and <latinform>nâ-kông</latinform> 'ear' (< <xref ref="820">#820</xref>).</par>
<par>Meithei <latinform>ə ŋaŋ</latinform> 'child' (cf. Lhoba <latinform>aŋaː</latinform>, Yi (Dafang) <latinform>a³³ŋa⁵⁵</latinform>, as well as set <xref ref="90">#90</xref>).</par>
<par>Note that the Sulong form has the same <latinform>mə-</latinform> prefix as in Meithei.</par>
<par>Meithei <latinform>-ŋ</latinform> perhaps result of assimilation to the syllable initial.</par>
